sections.paragraph:
προσμένω fut. προσμενῶ LXX; 1 aor. προσέμεινα; pf. inf. προσμεμενηκέναι (Just., D. 107, 2) (Pind., Hdt.+; ins; UPZ 60, 16 [168 b.c.]; LXX; GrBar 13:3; SibOr 5, 131; Jos., Vi. 62; 63; Just.).

① to be steadfast in association, remain/stay with τινί someone or someth.

ⓐ w. dat. of pers.

α. lit. Mt 15:32; Mk 8:2.

β. transf. sense τῷ κυρίῳ remain true to the Lord Ac 11:23 (Jos., Ant. 14, 20 τῷ Ἀριστοβούλῳ).

ⓑ w. dat. of thing continue in ταῖς δεήσεσιν 1 Ti 5:5. τῇ χάριτι τοῦ θεοῦ Ac 13:43. τῇ προθέσει τῆς καρδίας πρ. ἐν τῷ κυρίῳ 11:23 v.l.

② to stay on at a place beyond some point of time, remain longer, further (Herodas 8, 3) ἡμέρας ἱκανάς Ac 18:18. ἐν Ἐφέσῳ 1 Ti 1:3.—M-M. TW.

fut. fut. = fut.

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

aor. aor. = aorist

  1. pf. = perfect

inf. inf. = infinitive

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

Pind Pind , V b.c.—List 5

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

UPZ UPZ = Urkunden der Ptolemäerzeit—Lists 4, 6

GrBar GrBar = Greek Baruch (Apocalypsis Baruchi Graece)—List 2

SibOr SibOr = Oracula Sibyllina, II–III a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

someth. someth. = something

dat. dat. = dative

pers. pers. = person(s)

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

transf. transf. = transferred

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

Herodas Herodas , a.k.a. Herondas, III b.c.—List 5

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 883
